response:
to store the value we must analyze the prime factors of the factorial.
***
we need to store the count of the digit five within the hundred factorial.
***
the number of factors of five is found by dividing one hundred by five and twenty five.
***
this calculation allows us to store the total exponent of five as twenty four.
***
since the denominator contains only powers of ten, we store the power of five.
***
the power of ten is fifty, so we store the difference between fifty and twenty four.
***
the remaining power of five in the denominator is twenty six after we store the result.
***
we must store the fact that all other prime factors are canceled out completely.
***
the final denominator is five raised to the power of twenty six to store the answer.
***
we have stored the solution as five to the power of twenty six for the fraction.
